payload.title: Unified Engine: alt-misuse — Elements other than image (Tag: IMG) should not have alt attribute
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `alt-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/alt-misuse`, `/engine/alt-misuse_success`, `/engine/alt-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/alt-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The alt attribute is used to provide a text alternative for images. It is not meant to be used on elements other than images and therefore will not be read using screen-readers.
**Remediation advice:** Use a screen-reader-only element to add the accessibility description of the element that was misusing the alt attribute and remove the alt attribute.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 2 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `alt-misuse` examples published at `/engine/alt-misuse_success` and `/engine/alt-misuse_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 4 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: aria-controls-has-reference — aria-controls should reference a valid element id
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `aria-controls-has-reference`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/aria-controls-has-reference`, `/engine/aria-controls-has-reference_success`, `/engine/aria-controls-has-reference_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/aria-controls-has-reference/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The element’s aria-controls points to an id that does not exist, or is not valid, breaking the link between the controlling element and the content it manages.
**Remediation advice:** Make the value of aria-controls exactly match an existing, unique id of an element on the page. Remove or update the attribute if the target element is missing or no longer relevant.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 4 fail

1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `aria-controls-has-reference` examples published at `/engine/aria-controls-has-reference_success` and `/engine/aria-controls-has-reference_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 4 pass, 4 fail atomic HTML files.

3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: aria-describedby-has-reference — aria-describedby should reference a valid element id
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `aria-describedby-has-reference`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/aria-describedby-has-reference`, `/engine/aria-describedby-has-reference_success`, `/engine/aria-describedby-has-reference_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/aria-describedby-has-reference/atomic-tests/`

## Flow Phases

Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If an element’s aria-describedby attribute points to an id that does not exist or is not valid, assistive technologies will not convey the intended description, causing users to miss important context.
**Remediation advice:** Ensure aria-describedby references an existing, unique id on the page. Remove or update the attribute if the target element is missing or no longer relevant.

payload.title: Unified Engine: aria-invalid-mismatch — Form fields should indicate validation errors to assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `aria-invalid-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/aria-invalid-mismatch`, `/engine/aria-invalid-mismatch_success`, `/engine/aria-invalid-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/aria-invalid-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Validation errors in forms must be exposed in a way that assistive technologies can detect and announce them, ensuring users who rely on assistive technology can identify and correct mistakes.
**Remediation advice:** To make sure that screen reader users are aware of form fields with invalid input, set aria-invalid="true". Remove the attribute once the value becomes valid.
**Fixtures:** 1 pass, 2 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `aria-invalid-mismatch` examples published at `/engine/aria-invalid-mismatch_success` and `/engine/aria-invalid-mismatch_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 1 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: aria-invalid-misuse — Only input fields with validation errors should be assigned aria-invalid="true"
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `aria-invalid-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/aria-invalid-misuse`, `/engine/aria-invalid-misuse_success`, `/engine/aria-invalid-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/aria-invalid-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Using aria-invalid="true" on an input field that does not have errors may cause confusion for users who rely on assistive technology, who may try to correct their already valid response.
**Remediation advice:** To make sure that screen reader users can accurately identify form fields with invalid input, set aria-invalid="true" only on input fields that have errors. Remove the attribute once the value becomes valid.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 4 fail

1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `aria-invalid-misuse` examples published at `/engine/aria-invalid-misuse_success` and `/engine/aria-invalid-misuse_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 2 pass, 4 fail atomic HTML files.

2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

title.to: Unified Engine: aria-labelledby-has-reference — aria-labelledby should reference a valid element id

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `aria-labelledby-has-reference`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/aria-labelledby-has-reference`, `/engine/aria-labelledby-has-reference_success`, `/engine/aria-labelledby-has-reference_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/aria-labelledby-has-reference/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Since aria-labelledby relies on valid id references, screen readers can only announce the label if the target exists. If the id is missing or invalid, the label will not be conveyed, causing users to miss important context.
**Remediation advice:** Ensure aria-labelledby references an existing, unique id on the page. Remove or update the attribute if the target element is missing or no longer relevant.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 3 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: article-misuse — Only elements that function as articles should be tagged as article regions

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `article-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/article-misuse`, `/engine/article-misuse_success`, `/engine/article-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/article-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Using an <article> tag on content that is not self-contained and that cannot stand on its own outside the context of the page, such as a blog post, news story, or forum entry, causes screen readers to announce misleading information about the purpose the content in relation to the main subject of the page.
**Remediation advice:** Assign role="presentation" to the failing element or use a different HTML tag that is more appropriate for the function of the element.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 4 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: background-image-discernible-image — Functional image displayed using CSS background properties should be tagged for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `background-image-discernible-image`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/background-image-discernible-image`, `/engine/background-image-discernible-image_success`, `/engine/background-image-discernible-image_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/background-image-discernible-image/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Functional images presented using CSS background or background-image properties should be marked up using role="img" so that they can be identified as images by screen reader users.
**Remediation advice:** Add role="img" to elements that present functional images using CSS.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 1 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: breadcrumbs-mismatch — Breadcrumb navigation region should have a label

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `breadcrumbs-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/breadcrumbs-mismatch`, `/engine/breadcrumbs-mismatch_success`, `/engine/breadcrumbs-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/breadcrumbs-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A breadcrumb region presents a trail of links showing the user’s current page in relation to higher-level pages on a site. Without a label, it may be announced by screen reades simply as "navigation", making it hard to distinguish from other navigation regions on the page.
**Remediation advice:** Assign aria-label="Breadcrumbs" to the navigation landmark that contains the breadcrumb links.
**Fixtures:** 7 pass, 4 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: breadcrumbs-mismatch — Breadcrumb navigation region should have a label
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `breadcrumbs-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/breadcrumbs-mismatch`, `/engine/breadcrumbs-mismatch_success`, `/engine/breadcrumbs-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/breadcrumbs-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A breadcrumb region presents a trail of links showing the user’s current page in relation to higher-level pages on a site. Without a label, it may be announced by screen reades simply as "navigation", making it hard to distinguish from other navigation regions on the page.
**Remediation advice:** Assign aria-label="Breadcrumbs" to the navigation landmark that contains the breadcrumb links.
**Fixtures:** 7 pass, 4 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `breadcrumbs-mismatch` examples published at `/engine/breadcrumbs-mismatch_success` and `/engine/breadcrumbs-mismatch_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 7 pass, 4 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

title.to: Unified Engine: button-mismatch — Buttons should be tagged for assistive technology

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule "Button Roles" and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `button-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/button-mismatch`, `/engine/button-mismatch_success`, `/engine/button-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/button-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If interactive elements cannot be identified as buttons, screen reader users may not realize the element is actionable, which can stop them from submitting forms, opening dialogs, or performing other intended actions.
**Remediation advice:** Add role="button" to the custom interactive element, or use a HTML <button> element.
**Fixtures:** 11 pass, 2 fail

3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `button-mismatch` examples published at `/engine/button-mismatch_success` and `/engine/button-mismatch_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 11 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

postconditions.to:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

title.to: Unified Engine: carousel-aria-live — Carousels should not be tagged as live regions

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `carousel-aria-live`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/carousel-aria-live`, `/engine/carousel-aria-live_success`, `/engine/carousel-aria-live_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/carousel-aria-live/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When carousels are tagged as live regions, screen readers will interrupt reading and announce every slide change, even when the user isn't interacting with the carousel or is in another section entirely.
**Remediation advice:** Remove the aria-live attribute and any live region roles, such as role="alert", from the carousel and its content.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 3 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: carousel-discernible — Carousels should have a descriptive label
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `carousel-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/carousel-discernible`, `/engine/carousel-discernible_success`, `/engine/carousel-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/carousel-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Carousels need a label so assistive technology announces them with a clear name, such as “Featured products carousel”. This ensures screen reader users know the widget’s purpose and can differentiate it from other carousels on the page.
**Remediation advice:** Provide a clear label for the carousel using aria-labelledby to reference a visible heading or assign aria-label to the carousel container.
**Fixtures:** 5 pass, 2 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: carousel-dragging-movements — Carousels that require dragging movements should provide alternative methods of operation with a single pointer
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `carousel-dragging-movements`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/carousel-dragging-movements`, `/engine/carousel-dragging-movements_success`, `/engine/carousel-dragging-movements_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/carousel-dragging-movements/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Carousels that require dragging movements should provide alternative methods of operation with a single pointer for example prev and next buttons or pagination.
**Remediation advice:** The nodes in the failed nodes indicate that this carousel may not be operable with a single pointer. If this is the case, ensure that the carousel can be operated using a single pointer. For example, provide “Previous” and “Next” buttons to navigate between slides, or pagination controls that allow direct access to specific slides.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 4 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: carousel-mismatch — Carousels should be tagged for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `carousel-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/carousel-mismatch`, `/engine/carousel-mismatch_success`, `/engine/carousel-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/carousel-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The carousel container should have a role so assistive technology treats it as a distinct section. Using role="region" with a label exposes the carousel as a named region, allowing screen reader users to recognize the purpose of the component and navigate to it efficiently.
**Remediation advice:** Assign role="region" to the carousel container. Make sure to provide a descriptive label using aria-label or aria-labelledby.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 4 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: carousel-next-slide-discernible — Carousel navigation next arrow should be labelled for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `carousel-next-slide-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/carousel-next-slide-discernible`, `/engine/carousel-next-slide-discernible_success`, `/engine/carousel-next-slide-discernible_failure`

---
**Rule description:** Carousel arrow buttons are essential for operating carousels. By design, carousels are difficult for assistive technology to handle. If the navigation arrows aren't accessible, carousels may be impossible for blind users to operate.
**Remediation advice:** When using arrow button to change the carousel slide to the next in order, include an "aria-label" or a screen-reader-only text noting this button will show the next slide.

**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/carousel-previous-slide-discernible-correctly`, `/engine/carousel-previous-slide-discernible-correctly_success`, `/engine/carousel-previous-slide-discernible-correctly_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/carousel-previous-slide-discernible-correctly/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If the Previous control (often marked up using an arrow symbol) in a carousel is not given a descriptive label, screen reader users may not understand that the control activates the previous slide or set of slides in the carousel.
**Remediation advice:** Assign aria-label="Previous" to the Previous carousel pagination control.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 1 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `carousel-previous-slide-discernible-correctly` examples published at `/engine/carousel-previous-slide-discernible-correctly_success` and `/engine/carousel-previous-slide-discernible-correctly_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 3 pass, 1 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: carousel-redundant-discernible-text — Carousels should be labelled properly without redundancy
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `carousel-redundant-discernible-text`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/carousel-redundant-discernible-text`, `/engine/carousel-redundant-discernible-text_success`, `/engine/carousel-redundant-discernible-text_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/carousel-redundant-discernible-text/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Unlabeled carousel areas are difficult for screen reader users because content gets hidden and shown unexpectedly. However, when labelled, the label should not be redundant.
**Remediation advice:** When using the "aria-label" or "aria-labelledby" element to describe the contents of the carouse, do not use the word "carousel" in it, as it is redundant when used with the "role" and "aria-roledescription" attributes. Using all three of these attributes, assistive technology users will understand that this is a carousel region and what it contains.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 1 fail

5. **Fixture count:** 6 pass, 1 fail atomic HTML files.

title.to: Unified Engine: carousel-slide-picker-discernible — Carousel pagination tabs should have accurate and descriptive labels

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `carousel-slide-picker-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/carousel-slide-picker-discernible`, `/engine/carousel-slide-picker-discernible_success`, `/engine/carousel-slide-picker-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/carousel-slide-picker-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If carousel pagination tabs are not given meaningful labels, screen reader users are unable to determine which slide or set of slides will be displayed when a tab is activated.
**Remediation advice:** Provide each pagination tab with an accurate, descriptive label using aria-label or aria-labelledby. For single-slide navigation, use labels like “Slide 1,” “Slide 2,” etc. For grouped slides such as product cards, describe the range of items (e.g., “Products 1 to 4,” “Products 5 to 8”) and include category context when useful.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 2 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: checkbox-discernible — Checkbox controls should have a label
payload.description:

**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/checkbox-discernible`, `/engine/checkbox-discernible_success`, `/engine/checkbox-discernible_failure`

---
**Rule description:** Screen readers rely on properly coded and associated labels to announce the purpose of a form field. A checkbox control without an identifiable label may prevent screen reader users from completing the form.
**Remediation advice:** Assign a label to each checkbox control using <label for> with a matching id (or by wrapping the <label> around the control), or using ARIA attributes such as aria-label or aria-labelledby.

payload.title: Unified Engine: control-field-visible-and-tabbable — Custom checkboxes, radio buttons and file upload fields should be accessible to assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `control-field-visible-and-tabbable`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/control-field-visible-and-tabbable`, `/engine/control-field-visible-and-tabbable_success`, `/engine/control-field-visible-and-tabbable_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/control-field-visible-and-tabbable/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers have built-in mechanisms to handle checkboxes, radio buttons, and file upload fields. By default, assistive technology does not support custom checkboxes, radio buttons, and file upload fields, and using those may prevent screen reader users from interacting with the fields and keyboard users from tabbing to the fields.
**Remediation advice:** If using standard LABEL and INPUT fields with custom CSS styling, ensure the checkbox, radio button, or file input is fully visible to assistive technology but visually hidden using opacity, width, height, and positioning (never use display:none or visibility:hidden). Alternatively, create a standard checkbox, radio button, or file input field available only for screen readers using the screen reader only technique, and hide the custom checkbox, radio button, or file input from screen readers using aria-hidden="true".
**Fixtures:** 8 pass, 9 fail

**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/custom-select-options-list-listbox`, `/engine/custom-select-options-list-listbox_success`, `/engine/custom-select-options-list-listbox_failure`

payload.title: Unified Engine: dialog-modal-focus — Keyboard focus should move into and lock inside active dialogs
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `dialog-modal-focus`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/dialog-modal-focus`, `/engine/dialog-modal-focus_success`, `/engine/dialog-modal-focus_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/dialog-modal-focus/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Dialogs that appear on pages without receiving keyboard focus immediately on interaction often leave users navigating content behind the dialog and make it difficult or impossible for keyboard and screen reader users to access the dialog itself.
**Remediation advice:** When a dialog opens, use JavaScript to place keyboard focus on the first interactive element within the dialog. If static content, such as lists, tables, or paragraphs, appears before any interactive elements and it needs to be perceived in order to easily understand the content, tabindex="-1" can be added to a static element at the start of the content to initially focus that element.
**Fixtures:** 1 pass, 1 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: dialog-modal-mismatch — Modal dialogs should be tagged for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `dialog-modal-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/dialog-modal-mismatch`, `/engine/dialog-modal-mismatch_success`, `/engine/dialog-modal-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/dialog-modal-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Content behind active modal dialogs should not be navigable, otherwise screen reader users may still encounter hidden or unrelated content, disrupting the intended workflow and making it harder to focus on the dialog’s purpose.
**Remediation advice:** Add aria-modal="true" alongside role="dialog" to indicate to screen readers that the dialog is modal and that content outside of it should be treated as inactive. Screen readers will recognize this and adjust their behavior to keep the user’s focus inside the dialog.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 4 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: dialog-modal-misuse — Only elements that function as dialogs should be tagged as dialog
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `dialog-modal-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/dialog-modal-misuse`, `/engine/dialog-modal-misuse_success`, `/engine/dialog-modal-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/dialog-modal-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)

Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)

---
**Rule description:** Marking up elements as dialogs without actual dialog behavior causes screen readers to announce a dialog, misleading users into expecting modal interaction and restricted reading order that do not occur.
**Remediation advice:** Remove role="dialog" from the non-dialog element or add role="presentation" if the HTML <dialog> element is used.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 2 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: duplicate-id — The id attribute should have a unique value
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `duplicate-id`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/duplicate-id`, `/engine/duplicate-id_success`, `/engine/duplicate-id_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/duplicate-id/atomic-tests/`

---
**Rule description:** The id attribute is a unique identifier in the DOM, and using the same value more than once creates ambiguity. This can cause styles and scripts to behave unpredictably, and may also disrupt label or ARIA references that depend on unique IDs.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure all id attributes on a page have unique values.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 1 fail

Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Figure elements are often incorrectly used to display images on the screen. Incorrectly using the figure tag, without providing a proper figcaption, adds unnecessary clutter to the screen reader user's experience.
**Remediation advice:** Standard images should be coded regularly without having a figure parent element. The figure tag should only be used for images you wish to provide additional visual descriptions for, like the date and time a photo was taken or other metadata. To make a figure tag non-cluttering for screen-readers, add the role=presentation attribute to replace it with another element like DIV. Alternatively, if you did intend to use the figure tag, make sure to add a figcaption tag with the additional metadata
**Fixtures:** 10 pass, 8 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `figure-discernible` examples published at `/engine/figure-discernible_success` and `/engine/figure-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 10 pass, 8 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: focus-not-obscured-footer — Focused elements should not be obscured by a sticky footer
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `focus-not-obscured-footer`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/focus-not-obscured-footer`, `/engine/focus-not-obscured-footer_success`, `/engine/focus-not-obscured-footer_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/focus-not-obscured-footer/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A sticky footer remains anchored to the bottom of the screen while the rest of the page content can be scrolled. If it is not offset from interactive elements, it can overlap and obscure the item in focus.
**Remediation advice:** When a page has a sticky footer, make sure that any element receiving focus is not fully covered by the footer. One way to achieve this is by using the CSS property scroll-padding-bottom.
**Fixtures:** 9 pass, 10 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `focus-not-obscured-footer` examples published at `/engine/focus-not-obscured-footer_success` and `/engine/focus-not-obscured-footer_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 9 pass, 10 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: form-context-change-warning — Interacting with form controls should not cause a change in context unless a user is notified beforehand
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `form-context-change-warning`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/form-context-change-warning`, `/engine/form-context-change-warning_success`, `/engine/form-context-change-warning_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/form-context-change-warning/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Interacting with form controls shouldn't automatically submit a form or cause any other change in context without notifying the user in advance. Form controls that cause a context change on input can disorient a user, since the behavior is not expected.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure that forms can be manually submitted via a submit button, or provide instructions that notify users of the expected behavior before they interact with the control.
**Fixtures:** 5 pass, 8 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: form-duplicate-id — Forms and form fields must have unique ID attributes
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `form-duplicate-id`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/form-duplicate-id`, `/engine/form-duplicate-id_success`, `/engine/form-duplicate-id_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/form-duplicate-id/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers rely on ID attributes to be unique in order to announce to the users the correct content. If IDs are not unique, screen readers won't know which element is the correct one.
**Remediation advice:** Change the ID of the duplicate fields and forms so they are unique.
**Fixtures:** 1 pass, 3 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `form-duplicate-id` examples published at `/engine/form-duplicate-id_success` and `/engine/form-duplicate-id_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 1 pass, 3 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: form-mismatch — A container of input elements should be tagged as a form
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `form-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/form-mismatch`, `/engine/form-mismatch_success`, `/engine/form-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/form-mismatch/atomic-tests/`

payload.description:

title.to: Unified Engine: heading-discernible — Headings should not be empty

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule "Empty Headings" and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `heading-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/heading-discernible`, `/engine/heading-discernible_success`, `/engine/heading-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/heading-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Empty heading elements break the document outline, making navigation by headings less effective for screen reader users and causing confusion due to the disrupted page hierarchy.
**Remediation advice:** Remove empty HTML heading elements or assign aria-hidden="true" to make sure that they are ignored by screen readers.
**Fixtures:** 8 pass, 3 fail

preconditions.from: **Load the example website:** [https://jayquake.github.io/#/headings/empty-headings_success](https://jayquake.github.io/#/headings/empty-headings_success)
preconditions.to:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `heading-discernible` examples published at `/engine/heading-discernible_success` and `/engine/heading-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 8 pass, 3 fail atomic HTML files.

postconditions.to:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: heading-mismatch — Headings should be tagged for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `heading-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/heading-mismatch`, `/engine/heading-mismatch_success`, `/engine/heading-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/heading-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Text that visually functions as a heading for sighted users should also be tagged as a heading for screen reader users.
**Remediation advice:** If you specifically coded headings without native HTML tags (like h1, h2, etc.) for SEO reasons or otherwise, use role="heading" and aria-level="1/2/3/4/5/6" (the appropriate heading level) to make sure that screen readers announce them as headings. For example, role="heading" aria-level="3" will be treated by screen readers as a h3 heading. Alternatively, you can directly code those elements as native HTML headings.
**Fixtures:** 7 pass, 1 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: heading-misuse — Only elements that function as headings should be tagged as heading
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `heading-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/heading-misuse`, `/engine/heading-misuse_success`, `/engine/heading-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/heading-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Accurate tagging allows screen readers to present content in a logical structure. Misidentifying an element as a heading disrupts navigation, creating confusion about the importance of content and page hierarchy.
**Remediation advice:** Remove ARIA heading attributes from the failing element, or if the element is marked up using a native HTML tag, update the role according to the function of the element.
**Fixtures:** 9 pass, 5 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: heading-order — Heading levels should follow a logical hierarchy
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `heading-order`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/heading-order`, `/engine/heading-order_success`, `/engine/heading-order_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/heading-order/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Incorrect heading hierarchy can create a misleading page outline, disrupt navigation by heading shortcuts, and cause confusion about the structure and importance of content. Skipping levels or using them inconsistently makes it harder for screen reader users and others who rely on structured navigation to understand the page.
**Remediation advice:** Use heading levels in a meaningful, hierarchical order that reflects the structure of the content. Start with a single <h1> for the main page title, then use <h2> for primary sections, <h3> for subsections, and so on. Best practice avoids skipping levels (for example, jumping from <h1> to <h3>) to maintain a logical sequence and ensure a clear, consistent outline for all users.
**Fixtures:** 8 pass, 7 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: heading-order-optimal — Heading levels should reflect the structure of the content
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `heading-order-optimal`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/heading-order-optimal`, `/engine/heading-order-optimal_success`, `/engine/heading-order-optimal_failure`

---
**Rule description:** Incorrect heading hierarchy can create a misleading page outline, disrupt navigation by heading shortcuts, and cause confusion about the structure and importance of content. Using the correct heading level preserves a logical structure, allowing users to understand how sections relate to each other.
**Remediation advice:** Heading levels should reflect how each section relates to the main topic. Use <h1> for the overall subject,<h2> for major sections, and deeper levels, such as <h3> and <h4>, for subsections.

payload.title: Unified Engine: heading-single-h1 — A page should not have more than one main heading
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `heading-single-h1`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/heading-single-h1`, `/engine/heading-single-h1_success`, `/engine/heading-single-h1_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/heading-single-h1/atomic-tests/`

---
**Rule description:** Each web page should have only one h1 element so that screen reader users can identify the main topic or purpose of the page. Multiple h1s can confuse users by suggesting there are multiple primary topics, disrupting navigation by headings and making it harder to understand the overall page structure.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure that each web page has exactly one h1 element so that screen reader users can identify the main subject of the page.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 3 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: html-lang — Default page language should be defined
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `html-lang`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/html-lang`, `/engine/html-lang_success`, `/engine/html-lang_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/html-lang/atomic-tests/`

**Rule description:** Specifying a default page language ensures screen readers apply the correct pronunciation rules, voices, and braille output. Without it, screen readers may guess the language incorrectly, causing mispronunciations, confusion, and reduced comprehension for users.

payload.description:

**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/html-lang-valid`, `/engine/html-lang-valid_success`, `/engine/html-lang-valid_failure`

payload.title: Unified Engine: icon-discernible — Meaningful icons should have a label, while decorative icons should be hidden
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `icon-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/icon-discernible`, `/engine/icon-discernible_success`, `/engine/icon-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/icon-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Smaller graphics used as decorative or complementary elements, such as icons, and that do not provide additional information will often add unnecessary clutter to a screen reader user's browsing experience.
**Remediation advice:** If the image is meaningful, assign a text alternative with a description of the image content using the alt attribute for image elements, or aria-label for elements with role="img". If the image is decorative, provide an empty alt attribute for image elements, or role="presentation" for elements with role="img".
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 3 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `icon-discernible` examples published at `/engine/icon-discernible_success` and `/engine/icon-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 6 pass, 3 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: image-discernible — Functional images should have a text alternative
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `image-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/image-discernible`, `/engine/image-discernible_success`, `/engine/image-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/image-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Images require a text alternative when the image conveys meaningful content or serves a functional purpose. If the image is decorative, it must be hidden from assistive technology.
**Remediation advice:** If the image is meaningful, assign a text alternative with a description of the image content using the alt attribute for image elements, or aria-label for elements with role="img". If the image is decorative, provide an empty alt attribute for image elements, or role="presentation" for elements with role="img".
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 3 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: image-discernible-correctly — Functional images should have an informative and accurate text alternative
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `image-discernible-correctly`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/image-discernible-correctly`, `/engine/image-discernible-correctly_success`, `/engine/image-discernible-correctly_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/image-discernible-correctly/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Text alternatives must provide accurate descriptions of the image. Incorrect text alternatives, such as filenames or other placeholder values, may cause screen reader users to either miss essential information or hear unnecessary content that disrupts navigation.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure that the assigned text alternative describes the content or function of the image.
**Fixtures:** 10 pass, 11 fail

5. **Fixture count:** 10 pass, 11 fail atomic HTML files.

1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: input-discernible — Input controls should have a descriptive label
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `input-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/input-discernible`, `/engine/input-discernible_success`, `/engine/input-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/input-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers rely on correctly coded and associated labels to announce the purpose of a form field. If a label isn't properly associated with its input field, screen reader users won't know the expected input.
**Remediation advice:** Provide a <label> element and associate it with the <input> using the for and id attributes. Alternatively, you can assign an aria-label attribute directly to the <input> element.
**Fixtures:** 13 pass, 4 fail

1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `input-discernible` examples published at `/engine/input-discernible_success` and `/engine/input-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 13 pass, 4 fail atomic HTML files.

payload.title: Unified Engine: interactive-target-size — Targets for interactive elements should meet a minimum size (at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels) or have enough spacing around them
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `interactive-target-size`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/interactive-target-size`, `/engine/interactive-target-size_success`, `/engine/interactive-target-size_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/interactive-target-size/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Interactive elements, such as buttons and links, should have a minimum size of 24 by 24 CSS pixels or have enough spacing to avoid accidental activation of adjacent elements. This is crucial for users with motor impairments or those using the website in unstable environments, like on a bus.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure all interactive targets meet the minimum size of 24 by 24 CSS pixels. If resizing is not possible, provide spacing with a diameter of at least 24 CSS pixels around smaller targets to ensure they don't intersect with adjacent controls.
**Fixtures:** 13 pass, 10 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `interactive-target-size` examples published at `/engine/interactive-target-size_success` and `/engine/interactive-target-size_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 13 pass, 10 fail atomic HTML files.

payload.title: Unified Engine: letter-spacing-positive — Letter spacing should scale to at least 0.12 times the font size without loss of content or functionality
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `letter-spacing-positive`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/letter-spacing-positive`, `/engine/letter-spacing-positive_success`, `/engine/letter-spacing-positive_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/letter-spacing-positive/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When letter spacing is increased to 0.12 times the font size, text may be clipped, overlap, or overflow its container if styles like fixed widths, fixed heights, hidden overflow, or forced no-wrap prevent proper reflow.
**Remediation advice:** Use flexible heights and widths, position elements in ways that allow text reflow, enable text wrapping, and avoid hidden overflow to ensure text remains readable when letter spacing is scaled to 0.12 times the font size.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 2 fail

payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `link-anchor-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/link-anchor-discernible`, `/engine/link-anchor-discernible_success`, `/engine/link-anchor-discernible_failure`

---
**Rule description:** Activating anchor links enables users to navigate to a different section within the same page by scrolling and moving keyboard focus. Anchor links that do not contain visible text or labeled images should be assigned labels that inform screen reader users of their destination.

payload.title: Unified Engine: link-context — Links should indicate their functionality and destination to assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `link-context`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/link-context`, `/engine/link-context_success`, `/engine/link-context_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/link-context/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Visual cues on pages provide context to links or hint functionality to users with sight, even if the links aren't fully descriptive. Screen reader users need to know where a link will take them based on the anchor text without additional context.
**Remediation advice:** Examples include links to the shopping cart, to the company's Facebook page, to the site's search page, or to other similar destinations. If the link's anchor text doesn't explicitly describe those destinations, add screen-reader only text describing the destination

payload.title: Unified Engine: link-current-page — Visual indication that a link's destination is the current page should be announced by screen readers
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `link-current-page`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/link-current-page`, `/engine/link-current-page_success`, `/engine/link-current-page_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/link-current-page/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Visual cues are often used by sighted users to indicate which link represents the current page within a set of links. This information should be made available to screen reader users by assigning aria-current='page' to the link.

**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/link-homepage-warning`, `/engine/link-homepage-warning_success`, `/engine/link-homepage-warning_failure`

payload.title: Unified Engine: link-navigation-ambiguous — Link context should be exposed to assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `link-navigation-ambiguous`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/link-navigation-ambiguous`, `/engine/link-navigation-ambiguous_success`, `/engine/link-navigation-ambiguous_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/link-navigation-ambiguous/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen reader users may find it difficult to distinguish between links when the purpose of each link cannot be determined from its text alone or together with its immediate context.
**Remediation advice:** Assign a unique id to an existing element that contains additional context for the link, add aria-describedby to the link and reference the assigned id. Alternatively, nest a visibly hidden element that contains additional context inside of the link.
**Fixtures:** 10 pass, 6 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `link-navigation-ambiguous` examples published at `/engine/link-navigation-ambiguous_success` and `/engine/link-navigation-ambiguous_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 10 pass, 6 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: link-navigation-discernible — Navigation links should have a descriptive label
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `link-navigation-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/link-navigation-discernible`, `/engine/link-navigation-discernible_success`, `/engine/link-navigation-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/link-navigation-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Activating navigation links enables users to navigate to a different page within the site. Links that do not contain visible text or labeled images should be assigned labels that inform screen reader users of their destination.
**Remediation advice:** If a navigation link does not contain a labeled image or visible text, assign an aria-label that describes the destination of the link.
**Fixtures:** 7 pass, 3 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: link-new-window-warning — Warning a user when a link triggers a new browser window is recommended
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `link-new-window-warning`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/link-new-window-warning`, `/engine/link-new-window-warning_success`, `/engine/link-new-window-warning_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/link-new-window-warning/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** It's good practice to warn users about the expected behavior when activating a link triggers a new window.
**Remediation advice:** Add a visibly hidden text element that contains 'Opens new window'. Assign a unique id attribute to the element and add aria-describedby to the link, referencing the text element's id. Alternatively, nest a visibly hidden element that contains additional context inside of the link.
**Fixtures:** 7 pass, 2 fail

2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.

5. **Fixture count:** 7 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `list-not-empty`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/list-not-empty`, `/engine/list-not-empty_success`, `/engine/list-not-empty_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/list-not-empty/atomic-tests/`

---
**Rule description:** An empty list will still be announced by screen readers, which may confuse users, leaving them unsure if the list is empty or an issue prevents the screen reader from announcing the list items.

payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `menu-item-avoid`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/menu-item-avoid`, `/engine/menu-item-avoid_success`, `/engine/menu-item-avoid_failure`

---
**Rule description:** In most cases, using ARIA menu roles within a web page can negatively impact screen reader users, especially those using JAWS. role="menuitem" should be used for menu items in menu types that function like those found in desktop applications.
**Remediation advice:** Use standard lists with links and buttons (or custom buttons with role="button") and remove role="menuitem".

title.to: Unified Engine: menu-trigger-clickable — ARIA relationship and state attributes should only be applied to elements with appropriate roles

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule "Broken Submenu Indication" and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `menu-trigger-clickable`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/menu-trigger-clickable`, `/engine/menu-trigger-clickable_success`, `/engine/menu-trigger-clickable_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/menu-trigger-clickable/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Interactive elements that trigger additional content should only have relationship and state ARIA attributes, such as aria-expanded and aria-controls, if they have interactive roles, such as button, tab, combobox and in rarer cases, link.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure relationship and state ARIA attributes are only applied to elements with interactive roles, such as button, tab, and combobox. In rare cases, aria-expanded can be applied to link elements or elements with role='link', however this should only be done when the link both navigates and expands content.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 2 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: menu-trigger-correct-state — Triggers that expand additional content should expose their state to assistive technology

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `menu-trigger-correct-state`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state`, `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_success`, `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/menu-trigger-correct-state/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When components such as accordions or navigation menus do not expose their state, screen reader users may not realize that additional content can be revealed or know whether it is currently visible.
**Remediation advice:** Assign aria-expanded to common triggers, such as <button> or custom select elements to indicate whether their associated content is collapsed (false) or expanded (true). Ensure the value is updated as the state changes.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 2 fail

preconditions.from:

payload.title: Unified Engine: menu-trigger-correct-state — Triggers that expand additional content should expose their state to assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `menu-trigger-correct-state`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state`, `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_success`, `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/menu-trigger-correct-state/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When components such as accordions or navigation menus do not expose their state, screen reader users may not realize that additional content can be revealed or know whether it is currently visible.
**Remediation advice:** Assign aria-expanded to common triggers, such as <button> or custom select elements to indicate whether their associated content is collapsed (false) or expanded (true). Ensure the value is updated as the state changes.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 2 fail

1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `menu-trigger-correct-state` examples published at `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_success` and `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 6 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.

payload.title: Unified Engine: menu-trigger-misuse — Elements that do not expand additional content should not receive trigger attributes
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `menu-trigger-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/menu-trigger-misuse`, `/engine/menu-trigger-misuse_success`, `/engine/menu-trigger-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/menu-trigger-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Only interactive elements that trigger additional content should have relationship and state ARIA attributes, such as aria-expanded and aria-controls, if they have interactive roles, such as button, tab, combobox and in rarer cases, link.
**Remediation advice:** Remove ARIA relationship and state attributes that are reserved for triggers that expose additional content, such as aria-expanded or aria-controls, from the failing element
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 3 fail
