title.from: accessflow-engine integration: Aria label misuse / name-prohibited-no-aria-label
title.to: Unified Engine: name-prohibited-no-aria-label — aria-label should be used with compatible roles

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule "Aria Label Missuse" and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `name-prohibited-no-aria-label`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/name-prohibited-no-aria-label`, `/engine/name-prohibited-no-aria-label_success`, `/engine/name-prohibited-no-aria-label_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/name-prohibited-no-aria-label/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Using aria-label on elements that do not support the attribute can lead to the label being ignored, leaving users without the intended information.
**Remediation advice:** Use a visually hidden element to provide the name or description instead of an aria-label.
**Fixtures:** 21 pass, 16 fail

5. **Fixture count:** 21 pass, 16 fail atomic HTML files.

postconditions.to:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

A <span> with visible error text should not use aria-label. The screen reader will read the visible text and ignore the aria-label.

A

with visible error text should not use aria-label. The visible text already provides a description.
An empty <div> with an aria-label adds no value because there is no visible or functional content.

Failure: An empty <div> with an aria-label adds no value because there is no visible or functional content.

title.from: accessflow-engine integration: Navigation Tagging / navigation-discernible,  navigation-mismatch
title.to: Unified Engine: navigation-discernible — Navigation regions should have unique labels when there are multiple navigation landmarks

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule " Navigation Tagging " and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `navigation-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/navigation-discernible`, `/engine/navigation-discernible_success`, `/engine/navigation-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/navigation-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When multiple navigation regions exist on a page, each navigation element should have a unique label so that each region can be differentiated by screen reader users.
**Remediation advice:** Provide a unique label for each navigation region using either the aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute.
**Fixtures:** 10 pass, 4 fail

Data:
ruleId = navigation-discernible
issueSource = RAIDEN examples at /engine/navigation-discernible_success and /engine/navigation-discernible_failure

Scenario: navigation-discernible-failure 1: <nav> missing aria-label, aria-labelledby, or aria-describedby
navigation-discernible-failure 2: Empty aria-label
navigation-discernible-failure 3: aria-labelledby points to a non-existent ID
navigation-discernible-failure 4: aria-describedby points to a hidden element
navigation-discernible-failure 5: aria-label placed incorrectly on <ul>
navigation-mismatch-failure 1: <div> without role='navigation'
navigation-mismatch-failure 2: <section> used without role='navigation'
navigation-mismatch-failure 3: <article> used as a navigation landmark

payload.title: Unified Engine: navigation-discernible — Navigation regions should have unique labels when there are multiple navigation landmarks
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `navigation-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/navigation-discernible`, `/engine/navigation-discernible_success`, `/engine/navigation-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/navigation-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When multiple navigation regions exist on a page, each navigation element should have a unique label so that each region can be differentiated by screen reader users.
**Remediation advice:** Provide a unique label for each navigation region using either the aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute.
**Fixtures:** 10 pass, 4 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.

4. **Rule** `navigation-discernible` examples published at `/engine/navigation-discernible_success` and `/engine/navigation-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 10 pass, 4 fail atomic HTML files.

title.to: Unified Engine: navigation-item-link — List items in a navigation region should not be interactive

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `navigation-item-link`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/navigation-item-link`, `/engine/navigation-item-link_success`, `/engine/navigation-item-link_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/navigation-item-link/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Adding interactive behavior to <li> elements within a navigation list may prevent screen reader users from identifying the element as actionable, since <li> elements are announced as list items and expected to be static.
**Remediation advice:** In order to maintain the semantic structure of the list, a properly formed <a> element should be nested inside the <li>.
**Fixtures:** 7 pass, 7 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: navigation-mismatch — A list of navigation links is not contained in a navigation landmark
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `navigation-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/navigation-mismatch`, `/engine/navigation-mismatch_success`, `/engine/navigation-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/navigation-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers rely on accurate tagging and labeling to provide necessary context. If a navigation region is not tagged as a navigation landmark, screen reader users may lose orientation and find the page's structure difficult to understand.
**Remediation advice:** Add role="navigation" to the custom navigation region, or use a HTML <nav> element.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 6 fail

2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.

title.from: accessflow-engine integration: Misused Nav Tagging / navigation-misuse
title.to: Unified Engine: navigation-misuse — Navigation landmark does not contain key site navigation links

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule "Misused Nav Tagging" and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `navigation-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/navigation-misuse`, `/engine/navigation-misuse_success`, `/engine/navigation-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/navigation-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A navigation landmark should identify a section that contains primary links for moving through the site or page. Using navigation landmarks for minor or secondary link groups makes it harder for screen reader users to locate the page’s key navigation areas.
**Remediation advice:** Use navigation landmarks only for key navigation sections, such as the main site menu, table of contents, breadcrumbs, or pagination. Avoid using them for general link lists, social links, related links, or other secondary link groups, and keep the number of navigation landmarks as limited as practical.
**Fixtures:** 12 pass, 2 fail

3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `navigation-misuse` examples published at `/engine/navigation-misuse_success` and `/engine/navigation-misuse_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 12 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

payload.title: Unified Engine: navigation-not-nested — Avoid using nested navigation structure
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `navigation-not-nested`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/navigation-not-nested`, `/engine/navigation-not-nested_success`, `/engine/navigation-not-nested_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/navigation-not-nested/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Complex navigation components can be difficult for screen readers to handle. This is especially true if submenus or other complex structures exist. Nesting navigation regions makes it harder for screen reader users to understand a page's structure and disrupts orientation.
**Remediation advice:** If the failing element is a custom navigation region, remove role="navigation". If the failing element is coded using a HTML <nav> tag, apply role="presentation" or a role that is suitable for the function of the element.
**Fixtures:** 5 pass, 5 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `navigation-not-nested` examples published at `/engine/navigation-not-nested_success` and `/engine/navigation-not-nested_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 5 pass, 5 fail atomic HTML files.

3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: no-extra-information-in-title — The title attribute should not be the only method used to provide information
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `no-extra-information-in-title`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/no-extra-information-in-title`, `/engine/no-extra-information-in-title_success`, `/engine/no-extra-information-in-title_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/no-extra-information-in-title/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The title attribute is announced inconsistently across screen readers and browsers, making it unreliable for labeling interactive controls. Because many users may never hear the content, reliance on the attribute risks loss of information. Instead, it should be used to provide extra help text in addition to a valid label.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure that the title attribute is not the only means of providing a label by assigning an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute to the interactive control.
**Fixtures:** 13 pass, 5 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `no-extra-information-in-title` examples published at `/engine/no-extra-information-in-title_success` and `/engine/no-extra-information-in-title_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 13 pass, 5 fail atomic HTML files.

title.to: Unified Engine: no-role-application — Avoid using role="application"

description.from: Testing the behavior of the applications role and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `no-role-application`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/no-role-application`, `/engine/no-role-application_success`, `/engine/no-role-application_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/no-role-application/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Using role="application" is generally discouraged because it disables standard screen reader modes and forces users into an application mode. This removes familiar navigation shortcuts, such as heading or landmark navigation, and requires them to interact in ways they may not expect.
**Remediation advice:** Remove role="application" from the failing element.
**Fixtures:** 1 pass, 1 fail

to.data:
Data:
ruleId = no-role-application
issueSource = RAIDEN examples at /engine/no-role-application_success and /engine/no-role-application_failure

Scenario: A div is used as a container but improperly assigned role="application"
A main landmark improperly given role="application", overriding its native semantics.
A header element is incorrectly assigned role="application"
A content section with additional headings is wrapped with role="application".
An aside for complementary content improperly uses role="application".
A form used for user input is wrapped with role="application".
A table meant for data display is given role="application".
A figure containing an image and caption is given role="application".
A footer is improperly assigned role="application".
A dialog box is given role="application".
Expected Result: Screen readers should announce content normally, but the role="application" forces a custom interaction mode, confusing users.
Screen readers will not recognize the main region as a landmark, leading to navigation issues.
The header loses its semantic meaning, and screen readers may not navigate its content effectively.
The section is no longer recognized as a grouping element, causing confusion in screen reader navigation.
Screen readers cannot identify the aside as complementary, impacting user understanding of the page’s hierarchy.
Screen readers fail to properly handle input fields, potentially skipping them or reading them incorrectly.
Screen readers cannot identify table headers or row associations, making the data inaccessible.
The image and caption lose their semantic association, reducing the effectiveness of descriptions for screen readers.
The footer is no longer recognized as the page’s footer, impacting screen reader navigation.
The dialog’s focus management and interaction behavior are overridden, making it difficult to use with assistive technology.

scenario: pass: no element with role application (no-element-with-role-application.html)
fail: element with role application (element-with-role-application.html)

title.to: Unified Engine: page-meta-viewport-valid — Meta viewport should allow content scaling

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `page-meta-viewport-valid`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/page-meta-viewport-valid`, `/engine/page-meta-viewport-valid_success`, `/engine/page-meta-viewport-valid_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/page-meta-viewport-valid/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The meta viewport should allow scalability, typically with width=device-width, initial-scale=1, so text can be resized up to 200% without loss of functionality. Using user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1 prevents users from enlarging content, making it difficult for people with low vision to read or interact.
**Remediation advice:** Set content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" on the meta viewport element and avoid values like user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1 to preserve zoom and text scaling.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 4 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh — Pages should not use <meta http-equiv="refresh"> for automatic redirection or reloading
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh`, `/engine/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh_success`, `/engine/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A <meta> element with http-equiv="refresh" is sometimes used to automatically redirect users after a time delay. These timed changes can interrupt and disorient users who rely on assistive technology
**Remediation advice:** Remove <meta> elements with http-equiv="refresh". If timed user sessions are necessary, ensure users can extend the session time limit.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 11 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: popup-focus — Keyboard focus should move into and lock inside active dialogs
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `popup-focus`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/popup-focus`, `/engine/popup-focus_success`, `/engine/popup-focus_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/popup-focus/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Dialogs that appear on pages without receiving keyboard focus immediately on interaction often leave users navigating content behind the dialog and make it difficult or impossible for keyboard and screen reader users to access the dialog itself.
**Remediation advice:** When a dialog opens, use JavaScript to place keyboard focus on the first interactive element within the dialog. If static content, such as lists, tables, or paragraphs, appears before any interactive elements and it needs to be perceived in order to easily understand the content, tabindex="-1" can be added to a static element at the start of the content to initially focus that element.
**Fixtures:** 0 pass, 0 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: radio-discernible — Radio controls should have a label
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `radio-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/radio-discernible`, `/engine/radio-discernible_success`, `/engine/radio-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/radio-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers rely on properly coded and associated labels to announce the purpose of a form field. A radio control without an identifiable label may prevent screen reader users from completing the form.
**Remediation advice:** Assign a label to each radio control using <label for> with a matching id (or wrapping the <label> around the control), or ARIA attributes such as aria-label or aria-labelledby.
**Fixtures:** 11 pass, 6 fail

1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `radio-discernible` examples published at `/engine/radio-discernible_success` and `/engine/radio-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 11 pass, 6 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: radio-mismatch — Custom radio controls should be tagged for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `radio-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/radio-mismatch`, `/engine/radio-mismatch_success`, `/engine/radio-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/radio-mismatch/atomic-tests/`

## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.

Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)

---
**Rule description:** Screen readers have built-in mechanisms to handle radio components. By default, assistive technology does not support custom radio controls and using them without exposing the appropriate role may prevent screen reader users from interacting as expected with the component.
**Remediation advice:** Assign role="radio" to the custom radio control.

payload.title: Unified Engine: radio-misuse — Only elements that function as radio controls should receive role="radio"
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `radio-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/radio-misuse`, `/engine/radio-misuse_success`, `/engine/radio-misuse_failure`

---
**Rule description:** Setting role="radio" on elements that do not function as radio controls can mislead screen reader users by announcing them as selectable options in a group, causing unreliable navigation and interaction.

payload.title: Unified Engine: region-footer-mismatch — Global site information that appears at the end of each page is contained in a contentinfo landmark (footer)
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `region-footer-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/region-footer-mismatch`, `/engine/region-footer-mismatch_success`, `/engine/region-footer-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/region-footer-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The contentinfo region, typically represented by the <footer> element, is found at the end of each page and provides screen reader users with information about the website, such as copyright, contact details, legal information, and navigation links.
**Remediation advice:** Use a <footer> element or assign role="contentinfo" to the section that provides global information and consistently appears at the end of each page.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 1 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: region-footer-misuse — An element without global site information is tagged as a contentinfo landmark
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `region-footer-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/region-footer-misuse`, `/engine/region-footer-misuse_success`, `/engine/region-footer-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/region-footer-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When a region without global site information is tagged as a contentinfo landmark, screen reader users may be misled about its purpose and expect website-level details, such as copyright or contact information.
**Remediation advice:** If the failing element is a custom contentinfo region, remove role="contentinfo". If the failing element is coded using a HTML <footer> tag, change the tag to a <div> or an element with a suitable role.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 2 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: region-footer-single — Each page should include at most one global contentinfo landmark (footer)
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `region-footer-single`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/region-footer-single`, `/engine/region-footer-single_success`, `/engine/region-footer-single_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/region-footer-single/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Each page should normally include only one contentinfo landmark (usually the site footer) to keep landmark navigation simple and predictable. Additional contentinfo landmarks are permitted when clearly justified, but they must each have a unique accessible label.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure each page has only one top-level contentinfo landmark (footer). For additional footer-like blocks, nest them inside sectioning elements, or use role="region" with a label instead of contentinfo.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 3 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `region-footer-single` examples published at `/engine/region-footer-single_success` and `/engine/region-footer-single_failure`.

payload.title: Unified Engine: region-main-content — Each web page that uses landmark regions should have a main landmark
payload.description:

**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/region-main-content`, `/engine/region-main-content_success`, `/engine/region-main-content_failure`

**Rule description:** A main landmark (e.g., <main>) lets screen reader users quickly skip past repeated elements and jump straight to the primary content, improving navigation and orientation.

payload.title: Unified Engine: region-main-content-mismatch — All of the main content on the page is contained in the main landmark
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `region-main-content-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/region-main-content-mismatch`, `/engine/region-main-content-mismatch_success`, `/engine/region-main-content-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/region-main-content-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The main landmark represents the primary content of a page. It should include only content unique to that page and must remain separate from repeated elements, such as navigation, header, or footer.
**Remediation advice:** Avoid nesting <main> or elements with role="main" and other landmark or sectioning elements such as <header>, <footer>, <nav>, <article>, or <aside> (including elements with respective ARIA roles). Ensure that the main landmark contains all of the main content, and it is not enclosing the entire HTML document or positioned outside the primary content area.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 1 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: region-main-content-misuse — An element without main content is tagged as a main landmark
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `region-main-content-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/region-main-content-misuse`, `/engine/region-main-content-misuse_success`, `/engine/region-main-content-misuse_failure`

---
**Rule description:** Incorrectly tagging the main landmark may cause screen reader users to misunderstand where the primary content begins or ends, leading to confusion and inefficient navigation.
**Remediation advice:** If the failing element is a custom main landmark, remove role="main". If the failing element is coded using a HTML <main> tag, change the tag to a <div> or an element with a suitable role.

payload.title: Unified Engine: region-main-content-single — Each page should include at most one main landmark
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `region-main-content-single`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/region-main-content-single`, `/engine/region-main-content-single_success`, `/engine/region-main-content-single_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/region-main-content-single/atomic-tests/`

Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.

---
**Rule description:** A page typically presents one central subject, so a single main landmark establishes the boundaries of the primary content for screen reader users. Multiple main landmarks create uncertainty about the scope, leading to confusion and difficulty navigating the page.
**Remediation advice:** Keep only the true primary area as <main> or role="main", and change others to suitable elements—such as <section>, <nav>, or a neutral <div>. In modular or single-page apps, only the active view should expose a main landmark; remove the role or unmount inactive modules, or hide them with hidden or display:none.

title.to: Unified Engine: required-form-field-aria-required — Mandatory form fields should indicate that they are required

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule "Field Required" and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `required-form-field-aria-required`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/required-form-field-aria-required`, `/engine/required-form-field-aria-required_success`, `/engine/required-form-field-aria-required_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/required-form-field-aria-required/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If a field is marked as required only through visual cues, but lacks the required attribute or aria-required="true", screen readers will not announce it as mandatory. As a result, users may experience unnecessary delays or confusion when trying to submit the form.
**Remediation advice:** Add required or aria-required="true" to required input fields.
**Fixtures:** 5 pass, 4 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: sale-price-discernible — Original and discounted prices should be indicated to assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `sale-price-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/sale-price-discernible`, `/engine/sale-price-discernible_success`, `/engine/sale-price-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/sale-price-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Discounted prices often appear next to the original and distinguished with visual cues like strikethroughs or color changes. Both prices must also be conveyed by screen readers in a way that enables users to differentiate between the values, ensuring they can understand when a discount is applied.
**Remediation advice:** Add visually hidden text that explicitly identifies each value as the original price or the discounted price.
**Fixtures:** 8 pass, 5 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: search-form-mismatch — A search form should be tagged as a search landmark

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `search-form-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/search-form-mismatch`, `/engine/search-form-mismatch_success`, `/engine/search-form-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/search-form-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen reader users rely on landmarks to quickly access important regions of a page. Defining a form as a search landmark ensures that users can quickly recognize and navigate to the search form.
**Remediation advice:** Enclose the search form in a <search> element or assign role="search" to the <form> element.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 2 fail

5. **Fixture count:** 3 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

payload.title: Unified Engine: select-option-mismatch — Custom select options should be tagged for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `select-option-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/select-option-mismatch`, `/engine/select-option-mismatch_success`, `/engine/select-option-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/select-option-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Native select elements automatically expose each option to screen readers, however roles for custom components must be explicitly defined. If custom option elements are not given the correct ARIA role and nested according to markup and accessibility guidelines, assistive technology may not recognize or interact with them.
**Remediation advice:** Assign role="option" to each custom select option. Ensure that each option is directly contained in an element with role="listbox"
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 5 fail

1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.

4. **Rule** `select-option-mismatch` examples published at `/engine/select-option-mismatch_success` and `/engine/select-option-mismatch_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 4 pass, 5 fail atomic HTML files.

payload.title: Unified Engine: select-option-misuse — Only elements that function as select options should be tagged as option
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `select-option-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/select-option-misuse`, `/engine/select-option-misuse_success`, `/engine/select-option-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/select-option-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Assigning role="option" outside the required listbox structure causes screen readers to ignore the role, leaving users without the intended semantics of the original element. This can obscure key information and make the content harder for users to interpret.
**Remediation advice:** Remove role="option" from the failing element.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 6 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: skip-link-exists — Skip links are the preferred technique for bypassing repeated content

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `skip-link-exists`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/skip-link-exists`, `/engine/skip-link-exists_success`, `/engine/skip-link-exists_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/skip-link-exists/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Skip links let keyboard and screen reader users bypass repetitive navigation and jump directly to important sections such as main content, navigation, or footer. This improves efficiency, reduces keystrokes, and makes pages easier to use for those relying on assistive technology.
**Remediation advice:** Add skip links as the first focusable elements on the page, directing users to regions marked with unique ids and appropriate landmarks such as <main>, <nav>, or <footer>. Keep them visually hidden until focused so they don’t clutter the layout, but ensure they remain accessible to screen readers by avoiding aria-hidden="true" or CSS display:none.
**Fixtures:** 1 pass, 1 fail

payload.description:

### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Sliders that require dragging movements should also provide alternative ways to set their value, such as an option to tap or click anywhere on the track to set the value or an adjacent input field. This ensures users can operate the slider without relying solely on dragging. The rule checks whether there are input fields adjacent to the slider, and does not verify whether the slider itself supports tapping or clicking to set its value.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure the slider can be operated with a single pointer, or provide an input field that allows users to set the value directly. For example, a slider should allow users to tap or click anywhere on the track to set the value, or offer a numeric/text input beside the slider for direct value entry.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 1 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: sr-hidden-tabbable — Hidden elements should not be keyboard navigable
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `sr-hidden-tabbable`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/sr-hidden-tabbable`, `/engine/sr-hidden-tabbable_success`, `/engine/sr-hidden-tabbable_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/sr-hidden-tabbable/atomic-tests/`

## Flow Phases

Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Allowing hidden content to receive keyboard focus creates a confusing tab order, where keyboard users and screen reader users that navigate with the TAB key may encounter interactive controls that are unrelated to the current context.
**Remediation advice:** Use tabindex="-1" to remove elements from the tab order when they should not be exposed to assistive technology. Consider using CSS techniques, such as display:none or visibility:hidden when the content should be hidden from all users.
**Fixtures:** 5 pass, 2 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: tabbable-non-interactive — Non-interactive elements should not be keyboard navigable
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `tabbable-non-interactive`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/tabbable-non-interactive`, `/engine/tabbable-non-interactive_success`, `/engine/tabbable-non-interactive_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/tabbable-non-interactive/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Allowing static content to receive keyboard focus creates unnecessary stops in the tab order, forcing users to tab through elements that provide no action and making keyboard navigation less intuitive.
**Remediation advice:** Remove the tabindex attribute from the static element.
**Fixtures:** 7 pass, 1 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: tabbable-visible — Use the tabindex attribute correctly to manage focus for visible and hidden interactive elements
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `tabbable-visible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/tabbable-visible`, `/engine/tabbable-visible_success`, `/engine/tabbable-visible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/tabbable-visible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When interactive elements remain focusable while they are visually hidden, keyboard users may encounter controls that they cannot see and that should not be available in the current interface.
**Remediation advice:** Apply tabindex="0" to custom interactive elements so they follow the natural tab sequence, and tabindex="-1" when an element is offscreen or when it should only be reachable programmatically. Consider using CSS techniques, such as display:none or visibility:hidden when content should be hidden from all users.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 5 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: tabindex-valid — The tabindex attribute should be assigned a valid value
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `tabindex-valid`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/tabindex-valid`, `/engine/tabindex-valid_success`, `/engine/tabindex-valid_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/tabindex-valid/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Invalid tabindex values are ignored by browsers, while positive values override the natural focus order. Applying the tabindex attribute incorrectly can break the expected navigation flow for keyboard users.
**Remediation advice:** Restrict use of tabindex values to "0" or "-1", avoiding positive numbers and invalid value types, such as letters or decimals.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 2 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: table-column-header — Table column headers should be tagged for assistive technology

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `table-column-header`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/table-column-header`, `/engine/table-column-header_success`, `/engine/table-column-header_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/table-column-header/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If a column header is not marked up with the correct role or scope, screen reader users cannot determine which header applies to each cell.
**Remediation advice:** Use <th scope="col"> or assign role="columnheader" when building custom tables. Make sure each data cell is programmatically associated with the correct header so relationships are clear to assistive technology.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 3 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: table-column-header — Table column headers should be tagged for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `table-column-header`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/table-column-header`, `/engine/table-column-header_success`, `/engine/table-column-header_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/table-column-header/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If a column header is not marked up with the correct role or scope, screen reader users cannot determine which header applies to each cell.
**Remediation advice:** Use <th scope="col"> or assign role="columnheader" when building custom tables. Make sure each data cell is programmatically associated with the correct header so relationships are clear to assistive technology.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 3 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: table-headers — Tables should include table header tags
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `table-headers`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/table-headers`, `/engine/table-headers_success`, `/engine/table-headers_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/table-headers/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers can't match columns and cells without properly coded table header tags (TH). Without table headers, screen readers won't announce the table's content properly to users, leaving them unable to consume information.
**Remediation advice:** Add a THEAD element to indicate a table heading section, and include TH elements for every column, describing the purpose of that table column.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 7 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: table-misuse — Only elements that function as data tables should be tagged as table

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `table-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/table-misuse`, `/engine/table-misuse_success`, `/engine/table-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/table-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When a layout table is marked up with HTML elements like <table> or <tr>, or assigned table ARIA roles, screen readers announce a data table structure with rows, columns, and headers, even though the table is only used for page layout.
**Remediation advice:** Avoid using table markup or roles for visual layout. Use CSS for page structure, and if a table must be used for layout, remove semantic roles by adding role="presentation" or role="none".
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 8 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: table-misuse — Only elements that function as data tables should be tagged as table
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `table-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/table-misuse`, `/engine/table-misuse_success`, `/engine/table-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/table-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When a layout table is marked up with HTML elements like <table> or <tr>, or assigned table ARIA roles, screen readers announce a data table structure with rows, columns, and headers, even though the table is only used for page layout.
**Remediation advice:** Avoid using table markup or roles for visual layout. Use CSS for page structure, and if a table must be used for layout, remove semantic roles by adding role="presentation" or role="none".
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 8 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `table-misuse` examples published at `/engine/table-misuse_success` and `/engine/table-misuse_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 2 pass, 8 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: table-not-nested — Tables should not be nested
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `table-not-nested`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/table-not-nested`, `/engine/table-not-nested_success`, `/engine/table-not-nested_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/table-not-nested/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Nested tables are often misinterpreted by screen readers, making it hard for users to follow the intended structure and meaning of the data.
**Remediation advice:** Avoid nesting tables. Use separate tables with clear headings, and if tables are used for layout, assign role="presentation" or role="none" to each table element.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 5 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: table-row-header — Table row headers should be tagged for assistive technology

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `table-row-header`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/table-row-header`, `/engine/table-row-header_success`, `/engine/table-row-header_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/table-row-header/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If a table row header is not marked up with the correct role or scope, screen reader users cannot determine which header applies to each cell.
**Remediation advice:** Use <th scope="row"> or assign role="rowheader" when building custom tables. Make sure each data cell is programmatically associated with the correct header so relationships are clear to assistive technology.
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 3 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: toggle-button-misuse — Only elements that function as toggle buttons should be assigned the aria-pressed attribute
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `toggle-button-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/toggle-button-misuse`, `/engine/toggle-button-misuse_success`, `/engine/toggle-button-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/toggle-button-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The aria-pressed attribute is only announced by assistive technology when applied to button elements, including custom toggle controls with role="button". If it is used on elements with other roles, the attribute is ignored, leaving users unaware of any intended pressed or unpressed state.
**Remediation advice:** Remove the aria-pressed attribute from the failing element.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 1 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: user-rating-discernible — User ratings should be tagged and labeled for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `user-rating-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/user-rating-discernible`, `/engine/user-rating-discernible_success`, `/engine/user-rating-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/user-rating-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When a static star rating is presented using unlabeled icons, screen readers cannot interpret the symbols as meaningful content. Assigning a role and text alternative ensures the rating value is conveyed clearly, allowing non-visual users to understand the information.
**Remediation advice:** Assign role="img" with an aria-label, such as “4 out of 5 stars,” to the image element, or to an enclosing container if each star is presented as a separate element.
**Fixtures:** 8 pass, 2 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: visibility-mismatch — Visible content should not be hidden from assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `visibility-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/visibility-mismatch`, `/engine/visibility-mismatch_success`, `/engine/visibility-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/visibility-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If content remains visible on the screen but assigned aria-hidden="true", it will be excluded from the accessibility tree. As a result, screen reader users will not have access to the same information as sighted users.
**Remediation advice:** Remove aria-hidden="true" from visible elements. Make sure that the attribute is only used to hide redundant or inactive content.
**Fixtures:** 15 pass, 10 fail

title.from: accessflow-engine integration: Fake Hidden Content / visibility-misuse
title.to: Unified Engine: visibility-misuse — Visibly hidden content should not be exposed to assistive technology

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule *"Fake Hidden Content"* and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `visibility-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/visibility-misuse`, `/engine/visibility-misuse_success`, `/engine/visibility-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/visibility-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When elements are visually hidden but still exposed to assistive technology, screen reader users may encounter content that should not be available in the current interface. This can obscure the current state of the page and lead to confusion about what information or controls are available.
**Remediation advice:** Use aria-hidden="true" to remove elements from the accessibility tree when they should not be exposed to assistive technology. Consider using CSS techniques, such as display:none or visibility:hidden when the content should be hidden from all users.
**Fixtures:** 15 pass, 6 fail

to.data:
Data:
ruleId = visibility-misuse
issueSource = RAIDEN examples at /engine/visibility-misuse_success and /engine/visibility-misuse_failure

Scenario: Popup content is visible but marked hidden.
Modal content is hidden visually but not for assistive tech.
No visible space, but still accessible.
Just cause the class is "hidden" doesnt mean that the element is hidden from screen readers
HTML: No visible space, but still accessible.
Expected: **Failure Reason:** `display: none` is applied dynamically, but `aria-hidden="true"` should also be added to ensure full removal from screen readers.
**Failure Reason:** Content with zero width and height is still read by assistive tech.
>**Failure Reason:** Class-based hiding methods (`hidden`) do not necessarily remove content from screen readers. Proper `aria-hidden="true"` or `display: none` should be used.

payload.title: Unified Engine: visibility-misuse — Visibly hidden content should not be exposed to assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `visibility-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/visibility-misuse`, `/engine/visibility-misuse_success`, `/engine/visibility-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/visibility-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When elements are visually hidden but still exposed to assistive technology, screen reader users may encounter content that should not be available in the current interface. This can obscure the current state of the page and lead to confusion about what information or controls are available.
**Remediation advice:** Use aria-hidden="true" to remove elements from the accessibility tree when they should not be exposed to assistive technology. Consider using CSS techniques, such as display:none or visibility:hidden when the content should be hidden from all users.
**Fixtures:** 15 pass, 6 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `visibility-misuse` examples published at `/engine/visibility-misuse_success` and `/engine/visibility-misuse_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 15 pass, 6 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.
